stem: An L-shaped figure is formed by taking a $10 \text{ m} \times 6 \text{ m}$ rectangle and removing a $4 \text{ m} \times 2 \text{ m}$ rectangle from one corner. Find the area in m².
hint: Subtract the area of the removed rectangle from the area of the large rectangle.

stem: A composite shape is made of two rectangles joined together. One rectangle measures $7 \text{ cm} \times 3 \text{ cm}$ and the other measures $4 \text{ cm} \times 5 \text{ cm}$. Find the total area in cm².
hint: Find the area of each rectangle and add them together.

stem: An L-shaped figure is formed by taking a $12 \text{ cm} \times 5 \text{ cm}$ rectangle and removing a $3 \text{ cm} \times 4 \text{ cm}$ rectangle from one corner. Find the area in cm².
hint: Subtract the area of the removed rectangle from the area of the large rectangle.

stem: A step-shaped figure consists of two rectangles. The upper rectangle measures $8 \text{ m} \times 2 \text{ m}$ and the lower rectangle measures $5 \text{ m} \times 3 \text{ m}$. Find the total area in m².
hint: Find the area of each rectangle and add them together.

stem: A composite shape is formed by a $12 \text{ cm} \times 5 \text{ cm}$ rectangle with a triangle of base $12 \text{ cm}$ and height $4 \text{ cm}$ removed from the top edge. Find the remaining area in cm².
hint: Find the area of the rectangle, then subtract the area of the triangle.

stem: A composite shape consists of an $8 \text{ m} \times 6 \text{ m}$ rectangle with a triangle of base $4 \text{ m}$ and height $6 \text{ m}$ attached to one end. Find the total area in m².
hint: Find the area of the rectangle and the triangle separately, then add them.

stem: A $9 \text{ cm} \times 4 \text{ cm}$ rectangle has a right triangle with base $3 \text{ cm}$ and height $4 \text{ cm}$ removed from one corner. Find the remaining area in cm².
hint: Find the area of the rectangle, then subtract the area of the triangle.

stem: A composite shape is formed by a $10 \text{ m} \times 5 \text{ m}$ rectangle with a triangle of base $10 \text{ m}$ and height $3 \text{ m}$ placed on top. Find the total area in m².
hint: Find the area of the rectangle and the triangle separately, then add them.

stem: A composite shape is formed by a $10 \text{ cm} \times 6 \text{ cm}$ rectangle with a semicircle of diameter $6 \text{ cm}$ attached to one of the shorter ends. Find the total area to 2 decimal places in cm².
hint: Area of a semicircle $= \frac{1}{2} \pi r^2$ where $r$ is the radius.
explanation: $A_{\text{rectangle}} = 10 \times 6 = 60 \text{ cm}^2$. The semicircle has radius $r = 3 \text{ cm}$, so $A_{\text{semicircle}} = \frac{1}{2} \times \pi \times 3^2 = 4.5\pi \approx 14.14 \text{ cm}^2$. Total $\approx 60 + 14.14 = 74.14 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A square with side length $10 \text{ m}$ has a semicircle of diameter $10 \text{ m}$ removed from one side. Find the remaining area to 2 decimal places in m².
hint: Subtract the area of the semicircle from the area of the square.
explanation: $A_{\text{square}} = 10^2 = 100 \text{ m}^2$. The semicircle has radius $r = 5 \text{ m}$, so $A_{\text{semicircle}} = \frac{1}{2} \times \pi \times 5^2 = 12.5\pi \approx 39.27 \text{ m}^2$. Remaining area $\approx 100 - 39.27 = 60.73 \text{ m}^2$.

stem: A composite shape is formed by an $8 \text{ cm} \times 5 \text{ cm}$ rectangle with a semicircle of diameter $8 \text{ cm}$ attached to one of the longer sides. Find the total area to 2 decimal places in cm².
hint: Area of a semicircle $= \frac{1}{2} \pi r^2$. The diameter of the semicircle equals the length of the rectangle.
explanation: $A_{\text{rectangle}} = 8 \times 5 = 40 \text{ cm}^2$. The semicircle has radius $r = 4 \text{ cm}$, so $A_{\text{semicircle}} = \frac{1}{2} \times \pi \times 4^2 = 8\pi \approx 25.13 \text{ cm}^2$. Total $\approx 40 + 25.13 = 65.13 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A stadium-shaped figure is formed by a $6 \text{ m} \times 4 \text{ m}$ rectangle with a semicircle of diameter $4 \text{ m}$ attached to each of the shorter ends. Find the total area to 2 decimal places in m².
hint: The two semicircles together make one full circle. Find the area of the rectangle and the full circle, then add them.
explanation: $A_{\text{rectangle}} = 6 \times 4 = 24 \text{ m}^2$. The two semicircles (radius $r = 2 \text{ m}$) together form a full circle: $A_{\text{circle}} = \pi \times 2^2 = 4\pi \approx 12.57 \text{ m}^2$. Total $\approx 24 + 12.57 = 36.57 \text{ m}^2$.
